streams.reasoning: From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: phenylacetylene as dipolarophile kept separate to ensure controlled mixing with nitrile oxide precursor at reactor inlet. Concentration reduced from batch 0.5 M to 0.067 M to match calculated reactor concentration.

From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: ethyl nitroacetate as nitrile oxide precursor in separate stream allows precise stoichiometric control via flow rate ratio. 2:1 equiv ratio maintained by 2× concentration in this stream.

mixing_order_reasoning: From Chemistry Plan: Phenylacetylene and ethyl nitroacetate must be mixed rapidly at the reactor entrance via a T-mixer. The nitrile oxide intermediate generated from ethyl nitroacetate is transient and prone to dimerization if not immediately trapped by the alkyne. Rapid mixing ensures nitrile oxide encounters phenylacetylene before dimerization occurs.
post_reactor_steps: Product collection for offline workup with water extraction and dichloromethane
chemistry_notes: This is a thermal 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition proceeding via in-situ nitrile oxide generation from ethyl nitroacetate followed by [3+2] cycloaddition with phenylacetylene. The DES (TBAB/EG 1:5) provides high-temperature stability and may stabilize dipolar intermediates. Flow advantages include: (1) Superior heat transfer in 1 mm ID tubing enables rapid heating to 120°C, (2) Continuous generation/consumption of transient nitrile oxide minimizes dimerization, (3) Improved mass transfer in viscous DES. Calculations show Da = 1.97 indicating mixing may limit conversion - rapid T-mixer mixing is critical. BPR required to prevent DES component volatilization at 120°C.

reasoning_per_field.residence_time_min: Calculation estimated 15.0 min (range 1.0-22.5 min) from analogies. Analogy 3 used 20 min for [3+2] cycloaddition - chemically similar mechanism but different solvent system. Using 15.0 min as calculated optimum.
reasoning_per_field.flow_rate_mL_min: Calculation: total flow rate 0.5 mL/min (0.25 + 0.25) to achieve 15 min residence time with 7.5 mL reactor volume. Verified: τ = V/Q = 7.5/0.5 = 15 min.
reasoning_per_field.temperature_C: Same as batch 120°C. Chemistry Plan indicates this is optimal for nitrile oxide generation. Analogies show flow can handle high temperatures safely with BPR.
reasoning_per_field.concentration_M: Calculation shows reactor concentration 0.067 M (diluted from batch 0.5 M due to mixing). Analogy 1 used 0.5 M but different solvent - DES viscosity requires dilution for good mixing.
reasoning_per_field.BPR_bar: Calculation required 5.0 bar. DES components have high bp but phenylacetylene (bp 144°C) needs pressure at 120°C operation. Analogy 2 used 47.6 bar for 154°C - our 5 bar appropriate for 120°C.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_material: PFA selected for high temperature (120°C) compatibility and chemical inertness to DES. Not photochemistry so transparency not required.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_ID_mm: 1.0 mm from calculation for optimal heat/mass transfer in viscous DES. Calculation shows Re = 10.6 (laminar) and good plug flow (Pe = 173).
reasoning_per_field.reactor_volume_mL: 7.5 mL calculated from residence time and flow rate requirements. Analogy 1 used 30 mL but higher flow rate - our smaller volume appropriate for lower throughput.
